algebra.title: Solving for x using the Quadratic Formula
algebra.question: Solve for \(x\): \( 2x^2 - 5x - 3 = 0 \)

steps.note: Standard form is \(ax^2 + bx + c = 0\). Watch the signs!
Substitute carefully. Note that \((-5)^2\) is positive 25.
\(\Delta = 49\) is a perfect square, so the roots will be rational.
Always give both solutions unless stated otherwise.

algebra.title: Simultaneous Equations (Quadratic & Linear)
algebra.question: Solve for \(x\) and \(y\): \( y = x + 2 \) and \( x^2 + y^2 = 20 \)

steps.note: Substitute the linear into the quadratic. Don't forget to expand the bracket properly!
Bring everything to one side to get standard form.
Divide by 2 first to make factoring easier.
Pair your answers! \((-4; -2)\) and \((2; 4)\).
Start by finding the critical values (CV) by factoring.
These are the boundaries where the graph crosses the x-axis.
Since the parabola is concave up and we want 'less than zero', we take the 'valley' between the roots.

functions.title: Finding the Equation of a Hyperbola
functions.question: Find \(q\) and \(a\) for \( f(x) = \frac{a}{x-1} + q \) passing through \( (2; 5) \) with asymptote \( y = 2 \).

steps.note: The horizontal asymptote is always the value of \(q\).
Substitute the point (2; 5) into the equation.
Solve the linear equation for \(a\).
State the final equation clearly.
Swap \(x\) and \(y\) to find the inverse relationship.
Use the log definition: if \(x = b^y\) then \(y = \log_b x\).
Rename the result using inverse notation.

functions.title: Determining Nature of Roots
functions.question: For what values of \(k\) does \( x^2 - 4x + k = 0 \) have non-real roots?

steps.note: Condition for non-real (imaginary) roots is a negative discriminant.
Substitute into \(b^2 - 4ac\).
Solve the linear inequality.
If \(k\) is greater than 4, the parabola stays entirely above the x-axis.
Expand both parts using compound angle identities.
Watch the sign change when subtracting the second bracket!
The cosine terms cancel out.
Substitute special angle values to reach the simplest form.
DO NOT divide by \(\sin \theta\)! Always factorize to keep all solutions.
Solve each factor separately.
General solution for sine being zero.
General solution for sine being 0,5.
Double angle sine has 1 form. For cosine, pick the one that cancels the '+1'.
Simplify the denominator. The '1's cancel out.
Divide by common factor \(2\cos \alpha\) to reach RHS.

calculus.title: Derivative from First Principles
calculus.question: Find \( f'(x) \) if \( f(x) = 2x^2 \) using first principles.

steps.note: First, find and expand \(f(x+h)\).
Substitute into the limit definition formula.
Factorize \(h\) out and cancel it with the denominator.
Let \(h = 0\). You can check this using power rules!

calculus.title: Finding Equations of Tangents
calculus.question: Find equation of tangent to \( f(x) = x^2 - 4 \) at \( x = 3 \).

steps.note: Find the y-coordinate of the point (3; 5).
Differentiate and substitute x=3 to find the gradient \(m\).
Use the point-gradient form of a straight line.
Expand and simplify to standard line form.

patterns.title: Arithmetic Sequence to Series
patterns.question: The first 3 terms of an arithmetic sequence are \( 2x-4 ; 5x-3 ; 7x+2 \). Find \(x\) and the sum of the first 15 terms.

steps.note: For an arithmetic sequence, the common difference \(d\) is constant.
Always use brackets when substituting expressions with multiple terms.
Simplify both sides and solve the linear equation for \(x\).
Substitute \(x\) back into the original expressions to find \(a\) and \(d\).
Use the arithmetic sum formula \(S_n = \frac{n}{2}[2a + (n-1)d]\).

finance.title: Future Value Annuity (Saving)
finance.question: Thabo saves R500 at the end of every month for 5 years. The interest rate is 8% p.a. compounded monthly. Calculate his total savings.

steps.note: Identify the variables. Remember to divide the annual rate by 12 for monthly compounding.
State the Future Value annuity formula because payments are made to accumulate a future sum.
Substitute accurately. Do not round off the interest rate in your calculator!
Final answer should be rounded to two decimal places for currency.
